title: What is CSS @layer and how does it manage cascade priority?
description: Explain cascade layers for CSS specificity management.
options: @layer declares named cascade layers that control CSS rule priority regardless of specificity or source order. Layer order determines priority (last declared wins). Unlayered styles beat layered styles. Syntax: @layer base, components, utilities; then @layer base { ... }. Combines with @import to layer third-party CSS
correct_answer: @layer declares named cascade layers that control CSS rule priority regardless of specificity or source order. Layer order determines priority (last declared wins). Unlayered styles beat layered styles. Syntax: @layer base, components, utilities; then @layer base { ... }. Combines with @import to layer third-party CSS

title: What is the CSS Containment API (contain property)?
description: Explain CSS contain and its performance implications.
options: The 'contain' property limits the scope of browser recalculations. Values: layout (isolate layout), paint (clip painting to box), size (ignore children for sizing), style (counter/quote scope). content = layout + paint + style. strict = size + layout + paint + style. Enables browser optimizations by guaranteeing minimal reflow/repaint scope
correct_answer: The 'contain' property limits the scope of browser recalculations. Values: layout (isolate layout), paint (clip painting to box), size (ignore children for sizing), style (counter/quote scope). content = layout + paint + style. strict = size + layout + paint + style. Enables browser optimizations by guaranteeing minimal reflow/repaint scope

title: What is the Popover API in HTML?
description: Explain the native HTML Popover API.
options: The Popover API (popover attribute) creates native dismissible popovers without JavaScript. Elements with popover='auto' auto-dismiss when clicking outside or pressing Escape; popover='manual' requires explicit toggle. popovertarget attribute on buttons connects to popover by ID. Renders in top layer (above all z-indexes). Styleable with ::backdrop and :popover-open
correct_answer: The Popover API (popover attribute) creates native dismissible popovers without JavaScript. Elements with popover='auto' auto-dismiss when clicking outside or pressing Escape; popover='manual' requires explicit toggle. popovertarget attribute on buttons connects to popover by ID. Renders in top layer (above all z-indexes). Styleable with ::backdrop and :popover-open

title: What is the difference between CSS @container and @media queries?
description: Compare container queries with media queries.
options: @media queries respond to viewport/device conditions. @container queries respond to a parent container's size, enabling truly component-level responsive design. Requires container-type: inline-size on parent. Syntax: @container (min-width: 400px) { ... }. Named containers: container-name for targeting specific ancestors. Components adapt to where they're placed, not viewport
correct_answer: @media queries respond to viewport/device conditions. @container queries respond to a parent container's size, enabling truly component-level responsive design. Requires container-type: inline-size on parent. Syntax: @container (min-width: 400px) { ... }. Named containers: container-name for targeting specific ancestors. Components adapt to where they're placed, not viewport

title: What is the HTML <search> element?
description: Explain the semantic search element added to HTML.
options: The <search> element is a semantic landmark for search functionality, replacing the pattern <div role='search'>. It groups form controls and content related to searching/filtering. Accessible by default as a search landmark. Can contain forms, inputs, buttons, or search results. Helps assistive technologies identify search sections automatically
correct_answer: The <search> element is a semantic landmark for search functionality, replacing the pattern <div role='search'>. It groups form controls and content related to searching/filtering. Accessible by default as a search landmark. Can contain forms, inputs, buttons, or search results. Helps assistive technologies identify search sections automatically
